description: Add a UK visa application schema extraction and dynamic form-generation workflow to VIZA, modeled on the existing DS-160 approach. The run should identify the official UK visa application journey, capture required fields plus all enumerated options and conditional branches, normalize that into VIZA's visa_form_fields / schema flow, and expose a renderable UK visa form path with truthful documentation of any unsupported source-flow gaps.

scopeContract.truthStatement: This run is about official-source-first UK visa form parity: complete field coverage, accurate options, and preserved branching logic are more important than broad but speculative support.
scopeContract.rejectedAssumption: A hand-written UK form or a partially copied field list is NOT acceptable proof of parity. The implementation must be grounded in the official UK application flow and document any gaps explicitly.
scopeContract.designPrinciple: source-truth-over-manual-approximation: preserve the official field structure, requiredness, options, and conditional logic in a machine-readable VIZA schema before optimizing downstream automation.
scopeContract.integrationNotes: Reuse the existing DS-160/dynamic-form patterns where appropriate. Do not hardcode brittle one-off UI-only fields when the underlying schema path can be extended. Do not claim unsupported UK visa categories beyond the canonical v1 journey selected during implementation.

userStories.title: Pin the UK visa extraction scope and canonical source journey
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the repo scoped to one canonical UK visa application journey and its official source entrypoints, so Ralph builds against a concrete, reviewable target instead of a vague 'England visa' concept.
acceptanceCriteria: prd.json contains UK-001 through UK-005 for this run
The implementation names the canonical UK visa journey/source URLs used for v1
The scope contract explicitly prioritizes official-source parity over manual approximation
Any out-of-scope visa categories or source-flow ambiguities are documented rather than silently assumed
userStories.notes: First anchor the target before touching schema or UI.

userStories.title: Model the UK visa field inventory and branching rules in a normalized schema
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the UK visa journey represented as a machine-readable schema with fields, requiredness, options, and conditional logic, so VIZA can render and validate the form accurately.
acceptanceCriteria: A normalized UK visa schema artifact is added or generated in the repo
The schema captures sections/pages, field ids/names, labels, required flags, and option sets where applicable
Conditional or dependent questions are represented explicitly instead of being flattened away
Typecheck passes for any code/schema changes
userStories.notes: Prefer reuse of existing schema patterns over inventing a second incompatible model.

userStories.title: Wire the UK schema into VIZA's dynamic form path
userStories.description: As a user, I need the UK visa form to render through the existing dynamic form infrastructure, so UK applicants can complete the flow inside VIZA instead of relying on hardcoded pages.
acceptanceCriteria: The UK visa type/package is represented in the backend/frontend path needed for dynamic rendering
The dynamic form flow can load the UK schema and render representative sections/questions
Any required seed/mapping changes are idempotent and documented
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Keep changes surgical and aligned with existing visa_form_fields patterns if that is the active renderer source.

userStories.title: Add operator-visible gap reporting for unsupported UK fields or branches
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need unsupported or ambiguous UK source-flow gaps surfaced explicitly, so we do not silently ship an incomplete form while pretending parity is done.
acceptanceCriteria: The implementation documents or flags any unsupported source-flow gaps discovered during extraction
No missing-field uncertainty is silently ignored in code or docs
The repo includes a reviewer-usable artifact or note describing what was covered vs deferred
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Truthfulness matters more than pretending the first pass is perfect.

userStories.title: Document the UK visa form-generation workflow and next expansion path
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the repo docs and progress log to explain how the UK schema was derived, how to rerun or update it, and what the next category/branch expansion should be.
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation explains the chosen official source journey and how the UK schema/form path works
progress.txt is updated with implementation learnings and remaining blockers
The next recommended action after the initial UK form pass is stated clearly
Typecheck passes for any code/doc changes
userStories.notes: Close with operator-usable documentation, not just code changes.

userStories.title: Pin the EU Schengen Type C short-stay scope and canonical source form
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the repo scoped to the harmonized EU Schengen Type C (short-stay, up to 90 days in any 180-day period) application form (Annex I of EU Regulation 810/2009), so Ralph builds against a concrete, reviewable multilateral target instead of one member-state's portal.
acceptanceCriteria: prd.json contains SCH-001 through SCH-005 for this run
The implementation names the Schengen Annex I harmonized form as the canonical source
Country-specific embassy variations (fees, biometrics centres, appointment systems) are explicitly out of the schema and documented separately
Out-of-scope Schengen variants (Type D long-stay, FTD/FRTD transit) are documented rather than silently assumed
userStories.notes: Schengen is a multilateral visa — scope to the harmonized Annex I form, not any individual member state's portal.

userStories.title: Model the Schengen Annex I field inventory and branching rules in a normalized schema
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the Schengen Type C journey represented as a machine-readable schema with fields, requiredness, options, and conditional logic matching Annex I fields 1–37, so VIZA can render and validate the form accurately.
acceptanceCriteria: A new seed script scripts/seed-eu-schengen-c-short-stay-form-fields.ts exists with visa_type = EU_SCHENGEN_C_SHORT_STAY
The schema captures all Annex I sections (personal, travel document, EU family, contact, occupation, trip, accommodation, travel history, financial support, declaration)
Conditional/dependent questions (minors, EU-family member, sub-purpose fields, sponsor means) are represented explicitly via conditional_logic.showIf
Typecheck passes for agent-backend
userStories.notes: Prefer reuse of UK Standard Visitor patterns — umbrella purpose with sub-journey fields, repeatable groups for prior visas.

userStories.title: Wire the Schengen package into VIZA's dynamic form path
userStories.description: As a user, I need the Schengen Type C form to render through the existing dynamic form infrastructure, so Schengen applicants can complete the flow inside VIZA without any country-specific frontend code.
acceptanceCriteria: A Drizzle migration registers the EU_SCHENGEN_C_SHORT_STAY package in visa_packages (country = european_union)
The migration is idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING)
No frontend code changes are required — DynamicStepForm renders the seeded fields
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow UK-003 pattern exactly — DB rows only, no renderer changes.

userStories.title: Publish the Schengen gap report for unsupported fields or branches
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need Annex I fields, branches, and country-specific variations that are not fully captured surfaced explicitly, so we don't silently ship an incomplete Schengen form while claiming parity.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/schengen-visa-gap-report.md exists with coverage summary, known limitations (country variations, biometrics, fees), and reviewer checklist
Annex I fields deferred beyond v1 are documented with reason and workaround
Conditional-logic risks (nationality-gated fingerprint waivers, multi-value gates) are called out
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Truthfulness matters more than pretending the first pass is perfect.

userStories.title: Document the Schengen visa form-generation workflow and next expansion path
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a scope doc and progress log entry explaining how the Schengen schema was derived, how to rerun or update it, and what the next Schengen-variant expansion should be (Type D long-stay, FTD).
acceptanceCriteria: docs/schengen-visa-scope.md documents the Annex I source, canonical journey, and rerun steps
progress.txt is updated with implementation learnings, remaining blockers, and next expansion path
Recommended next actions (live-portal QA, Type D expansion, country-specific UX shims) are stated clearly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Close with operator-usable documentation, not just code changes.

userStories.title: Bilingual Review Page
userStories.description: As a user, I need to compare my original answers with the official English or formatted version before final submission, so I can confirm translations, passport-name spellings, and date formats.
acceptanceCriteria: The Review step automatically attempts Chinese-to-English translation when the user enters review
The review UI displays original answers and official English/formatted values side by side
Chinese text translations can be edited and saved through the existing application_translations PATCH endpoint
Date fields are displayed with their official schema format and sensitive name/place fields show passport-spelling warnings
Translation failure is visible and retryable but does not block final submission
Typecheck passes for modified frontend code
userStories.notes: Integrated into the dynamic Review step after standalone validation. Review now auto-attempts translation on entry, shows side-by-side bilingual rows before final submission, and keeps translation failure retryable/non-blocking. Standalone validation remains available at /bilingual-review-preview.

userStories.title: AI Field Guidance Co-Pilot
userStories.description: As a user, I need field-level guidance beside the application form, so I can see example answers, official warnings, and format hints when I am unsure about a question.
acceptanceCriteria: DynamicStepForm reports the active field context when a user focuses or clicks a field
A FieldGuidancePanel displays example answers, official warning text, formatting hints, sources, and confidence
A backend POST /api/field-guidance endpoint returns structured guidance from visa_chunks plus AI generation when available
The endpoint falls back to deterministic placeholder/options/validation guidance when AI or retrieval is unavailable
Guidance responses are cached by visa_type, field_name, and locale
Typecheck passes for modified frontend and backend code
userStories.notes: Next iteration. v1 should be field tips only, not a full chat sidebar.
